front: Poem: originally printed sideways to resemble flying creatures; lines contract when the speaker is 'most thin' and expand with spiritual health
Poem: shaped poem stating 'A heart alone is such a stone as nothing but Thy power doth cut'; the structure is 'made of a heart, and cemented with tears'
Work: collection of 160 'Sacred Poems and Private Ejaculations'; Richard Crashaw's Steps to the Temple is titled in homage
Poem: God pours a 'glass of blessings' onto man but retains only Rest at the bottom
Poem: opens 'I struck the board and cried, No more; I will abroad!'; the speaker is urged to 'forsake thy cage, thy rope of sands'
Poem: uses echo verse beginning 'O Who will show me those delights on high? Echo: I'
Poem: concludes 'So I did sit and eat' after the title figure enjoins the speaker to 'taste my meat'
